    "content": "     1\u2192<template>\n     2\u2192  <div class=\"app-container\">\n     3\u2192    <AppHeader class=\"app-header no-print\">\n     4\u2192      <template #right-actions>\n     5\u2192        <div class=\"export-area no-print\">\n     6\u2192          <div v-if=\"showDivergenceWarning\" class=\"divergence-warning\">\n     7\u2192            \u26a0 Some content may have been changed during formatting. Please review carefully before exporting.\n     8\u2192          </div>\n     9\u2192          <UButton\n    10\u2192            size=\"xs\"\n    11\u2192            variant=\"outline\"\n    12\u2192            icon=\"i-heroicons-printer\"\n    13\u2192            :disabled=\"!formattedXml\"\n    14\u2192            @click=\"handlePrint\"\n    15\u2192          >\n    16\u2192            Export PDF\n    17\u2192          </UButton>\n    18\u2192          <span class=\"export-hint\">Works best in Chrome. In the print dialog, choose 'Save as PDF'.</span>\n    19\u2192        </div>\n    20\u2192      </template>\n    21\u2192    </AppHeader>\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192    <!-- Dual-panel layout: Text input (left) and Preview (right) -->\n    24\u2192    <div class=\"dual-panel-layout\">\n    25\u2192      <!-- Left Panel: Plain text input -->\n    26\u2192      <div class=\"editor-panel no-print\">\n    27\u2192        <textarea\n    28\u2192          v-model=\"textContent\"\n    29\u2192          class=\"text-input\"\n    30\u2192          :placeholder=\"placeholder\"\n    31\u2192          :disabled=\"isFormatting\"\n    32\u2192        />\n    33\u2192        <div class=\"action-bar\">\n    34\u2192          <div class=\"type-selector\">\n    35\u2192            <button\n    36\u2192              class=\"type-btn\"\n    37\u2192              :class=\"{ 'type-btn--active': selectedDocType === 'resume' }\"\n    38\u2192              :disabled=\"isFormatting\"\n    39\u2192              @click=\"selectedDocType = 'resume'\"\n    40\u2192            >\n    41\u2192              Resume\n    42\u2192            </button>\n    43\u2192            <button\n    44\u2192              class=\"type-btn\"\n    45\u2192              :class=\"{ 'type-btn--active': selectedDocType === 'letter' }\"\n    46\u2192              :disabled=\"isFormatting\"\n    47\u2192              @click=\"selectedDocType = 'letter'\"\n    48\u2192            >\n    49\u2192              Letter\n    50\u2192            </button>\n    51\u2192          </div>\n    52\u2192          <div class=\"action-row\">\n    53\u2192            <UButton\n    54\u2192              :disabled=\"!textContent.trim() || isFormatting\"\n    55\u2192              :loading=\"isFormatting\"\n    56\u2192              @click=\"formatDocument\"\n    57\u2192            >\n    58\u2192              {{ isFormatting ? 'Formatting...' : formatButtonLabel }}\n    59\u2192            </UButton>\n    60\u2192            <p v-if=\"validationMessage\" class=\"validation-message\">\n    61\u2192              {{ validationMessage }}\n    62\u2192            </p>\n    63\u2192          </div>\n    64\u2192        </div>\n    65\u2192      </div>\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192      <!-- Right Panel: Preview -->\n    68\u2192      <div class=\"preview-panel\">\n    69\u2192        <!-- Skeleton loading state -->\n    70\u2192        <div v-if=\"isFormatting\" class=\"skeleton-wrapper no-print\">\n    71\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-name\" />\n    72\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-contact\" />\n    73\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton-divider\" />\n    74\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    75\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short\" />\n    76\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    77\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton-gap\" />\n    78\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\" />\n    79\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    80\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--medium\" />\n    81\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line\" />\n    82\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short\" />\n    83\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton-gap\" />\n    84\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-heading\" />\n    85\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--medium\" />\n    86\u2192          <div class=\"skeleton skeleton-line skeleton-line--short\" />\n    87\u2192        </div>\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192        <!-- Error state -->\n    90\u2192        <div v-else-if=\"formatError\" class=\"error-state no-print\">\n    91\u2192          <p class=\"error-message\">{{ formatError }}</p>\n    92\u2192          <UButton variant=\"outline\" @click=\"formatDocument\">\n    93\u2192            Try Again\n    94\u2192          </UButton>\n    95\u2192        </div>\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192        <!-- Empty state -->\n    98\u2192        <div v-else-if=\"!formattedXml\" class=\"preview-empty no-print\">\n    99\u2192          <p>Your formatted document will appear here.</p>\n   100\u2192        </div>\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192        <!-- Preview + review note -->\n   103\u2192        <template v-else>\n   104\u2192          <PreviewPanel\n   105\u2192            :xml-content=\"formattedXml\"\n   106\u2192            :zoom=\"1\"\n   107\u2192          />\n   108\u2192          <div class=\"review-note no-print\">\n   109\u2192            Your content has been formatted but not rewritten. Please review before exporting.\n   110\u2192          </div>\n   111\u2192        </template>\n   112\u2192      </div>\n   113\u2192    </div>\n   114\u2192  </div>\n   115\u2192</template>\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192<script setup lang=\"ts\">\n   118\u2192import { ref, computed } from 'vue'\n   119\u2192\n   120\u2192useHead({\n   121\u2192  title: 'OhMyDoc',\n   122\u2192})\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192const placeholder = `Paste your resume or cover letter here.\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192Example:\n   127\u2192Jane Smith\n   128\u2192jane@email.com \u00b7 Toronto, ON\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192Software Engineer with 8 years...`\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192const textContent = ref('')\n   133\u2192const formattedXml = ref('')\n   134\u2192const isFormatting = ref(false)\n   135\u2192const formatError = ref('')\n   136\u2192const documentType = ref('')\n   137\u2192const divergence = ref(0)\n   138\u2192const validationMessage = ref('')\n   139\u2192const selectedDocType = ref<'resume' | 'letter'>('resume')\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192const formatButtonLabel = computed(() =>\n   142\u2192  selectedDocType.value === 'letter' ? 'Format My Letter' : 'Format My Resume',\n   143\u2192)\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192const showDivergenceWarning = computed(() => formattedXml.value && divergence.value > 0.05)\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192function handlePrint() {\n   148\u2192  window.print()\n   149\u2192}\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192async function formatDocument() {\n   152\u2192  validationMessage.value = ''\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192  if (!textContent.value.trim()) return\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192  if (textContent.value.trim().length < 50) {\n   157\u2192    validationMessage.value = 'Please paste at least 50 characters of content to format.'\n   158\u2192    return\n   159\u2192  }\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192  if (isFormatting.value) return\n   162\u2192\n   163\u2192  isFormatting.value = true\n   164\u2192  formatError.value = ''\n   165\u2192  formattedXml.value = ''\n   166\u2192  documentType.value = ''\n   167\u2192  divergence.value = 0\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192  try {\n   170\u2192    const result = await $fetch<{ xml: string, divergence: number, documentType: string }>('/api/format', {\n   171\u2192      method: 'POST',\n   172\u2192      body: { text: textContent.value, docType: selectedDocType.value },\n   173\u2192    })\n   174\u2192    formattedXml.value = result.xml\n   175\u2192    documentType.value = result.documentType\n   176\u2192    divergence.value = result.divergence\n   177\u2192  }\n   178\u2192  catch (err: unknown) {\n   179\u2192    const raw = (err as { data?: { rawResponse?: string } })?.data?.rawResponse\n   180\u2192    if (raw) {\n   181\u2192      console.error('[OhMyDoc] Raw AI response (malformed XML):', raw)\n   182\u2192    }\n   183\u2192    const message = (err as { data?: { message?: string } })?.data?.message\n   184\u2192    formatError.value = message || 'Something went wrong. Please try again.'\n   185\u2192  }\n   186\u2192  finally {\n   187\u2192    isFormatting.value = false\n   188\u2192  }\n   189\u2192}\n   190\u2192</script>\n   191\u2192\n   192\u2192<style scoped>\n   193\u2192.app-container {\n   194\u2192  display: flex;\n   195\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   196\u2192  height: 100vh;\n   197\u2192  width: 100vw;\n   198\u2192  overflow: hidden;\n   199\u2192}\n   200\u2192\n   201\u2192.dual-panel-layout {\n   202\u2192  display: grid;\n   203\u2192  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;\n   204\u2192  flex: 1;\n   205\u2192  min-height: 0;\n   206\u2192  gap: 0;\n   207\u2192  min-width: 1024px;\n   208\u2192}\n   209\u2192\n   210\u2192.editor-panel {\n   211\u2192  display: flex;\n   212\u2192  flex-direction: column;\n   213\u2192 1024px) {\n   458\u2192  .dual-panel-layout {\n   459\u2192    grid-template-columns: 1fr;\n   460\u2192    grid-template-rows: 1fr 1fr;\n   461\u2192    min-width: unset;\n   462\u2192  }\n USERMESSAGE #64
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→import { Parser } from 'htmlparser2'
     2→
     3→const RESUME_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a professional document formatter. Convert plain-text resumes into structured XML.
     4→
     5→Use this schema:
     6→<document type="resume">
     7→  <header>
     8→    <name>Full Name</name>
     9→    <contact>
    10→      <email>email@example.com</email>
    11→      <phone>555-000-0000</phone>
    12→      <location>City, State</location>
    13→      <linkedin>linkedin.com/in/username</linkedin>
    14→      <website>portfolio.com</website>
    15→    </contact>
    16→  </header>
    17→  <summary>Professional summary text.</summary>
    18→  <experience>
    19→    <role>
    20→      <title>Job Title</title>
    21→      <company>Company Name</company>
    22→      <period>Month Year – Month Year</period>
    23→      <bullets>
    24→        <bullet>Key achievement or responsibility</bullet>
    25→      </bullets>
    26→    </role>
    27→  </experience>
    28→  <education>
    29→    <degree>Degree Name</degree>
    30→    <school>School Name</school>
    31→    <year>Graduation Year</year>
    32→    <note>Honors or relevant note</note>
    33→  </education>
    34→  <skills>Skill 1, Skill 2, Skill 3</skills>
    35→  <certifications>Cert 1, Cert 2</certifications>
    36→  <projects>Project descriptions</projects>
    37→  <volunteer>Volunteer experience</volunteer>
    38→</document>
    39→
    40→Rules:
    41→- Preserve ALL information from the source text
    42→- Omit empty optional tags
    43→- Output ONLY valid XML — no markdown fences, no explanation`
    44→
    45→const LETTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = `You are a professional document formatter. Convert plain-text cover letters or letters into structured XML.
    46→
    47→Use this schema:
    48→<document type="cover-letter">
    49→  <header>
    50→    <name>Sender Full Name</name>
    51→    <contact>
    52→      <email>email@example.com</email>
    53→      <phone>555-000-0000</phone>
    54→      <location>City, State</location>
    55→    </contact>
    56→  </header>
    57→  <date>Month Day, Year</date>
    58→  <recipient>
    59→    <name>Recipient Name</name>
    60→    <title>Recipient Title</title>
    61→    <company>Company Name</company>
    62→    <address>City, State</address>
    63→  </recipient>
    64→  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>
    65→  <body>
    66→    <paragraph>Opening paragraph text.</paragraph>
    67→    <paragraph>Body paragraph text.</paragraph>
    68→    <paragraph>Closing paragraph text.</paragraph>
    69→  </body>
    70→  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>
    71→  <signature>Sender Full Name</signature>
    72→</document>
    73→
    74→Rules:
    75→- Preserve ALL information from the source text
    76→- Split letter body into logical paragraphs
    77→- Omit empty optional tags (e.g. omit <date> if no date found, omit recipient <name> if unknown)
    78→- Output ONLY valid XML — no markdown fences, no explanation`
    79→
    80→interface OpenAIChatResponse {
    81→  choices: Array<{
    82→    message: {
    83→      content: string
    84→    }
    85→  }>
    86→}
    87→
    88→function extractTextFromXml(xmlString: string): string {
    89→  const textParts: string[] = []
    90→  const parser = new Parser({
    91→    ontext(text) {
    92→      textParts.push(text)
    93→    },
    94→  }, { decodeEntities: true })
    95→  parser.write(xmlString)
    96→  parser.end()
    97→  return textParts.join(' ')
    98→}
    99→
   100→function detectDocumentType(xmlString: string): string {
   101→  const match = xmlString.match(/<document[^>]+type="([^"]+)"/)
   102→  return match?.[1] ?? 'resume'
   103→}
   104→
   105→function validateXmlStructure(xmlString: string): boolean {
   106→  let valid = true
   107→  let depth = 0
   108→  const parser = new Parser({
   109→    onerror() {
   110→      valid = false
   111→    },
   112→    onopentag() {
   113→      depth++
   114→    },
   115→    onclosetag() {
   116→      depth--
   117→      if (depth < 0) valid = false
   118→    },
   119→  }, { xmlMode: true })
   120→  parser.write(xmlString)
   121→  parser.end()
   122→  return valid && depth === 0
   123→}
   124→
   125→export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
   126→  const body = await readBody(event)
   127→
   128→  if (!body.text || body.text.length < 50) {
   129→    throw createError({ statusCode: 400, message: 'Paste more content to format.' })
   130→  }
   131→
   132→  const config = useRuntimeConfig()
   133→
   134→  if (!config.openaiApiKey) {
   135→    throw createError({ statusCode: 500, message: 'OpenAI API key is not configured.' })
   136→  }
   137→
   138→  const inputLen = body.text.length
   139→
   140→  // Select system prompt based on client-supplied docType; fall back to resume
   141→  const docType: string = body.docType === 'letter' ? 'letter' : 'resume'
   142→  const systemPrompt = docType === 'letter' ? LETTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT : RESUME_SYSTEM_PROMPT
   143→
   144→  const response = await $fetch<OpenAIChatResponse>('https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions', {
   145→    method: 'POST',
   146→    headers: {
   147→      'Authorization': `Bearer ${config.openaiApiKey}`,
   148→      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
   149→    },
   150→    body: {
   151→      model: 'gpt-4o-mini',
   152→      messages: [
   153→        { role: 'system', content: systemPrompt },
   154→        { role: 'user', content: body.text },
   155→      ],
   156→      temperature: 0.3,
   157→      max_tokens: 4000,
   158→    },
   159→  })
   160→
   161→  const raw = response.choices[0]?.message?.content ?? ''
   162→
   163→  // Strip markdown fences if present
   164→  const xml = raw
   165→    .replace(/^```(?:xml)?
?/m, '')
   166→    .replace(/
?```$/m, '')
   167→    .trim()
   168→
   169→  if (!validateXmlStructure(xml)) {
   170→    throw createError({ statusCode: 502, message: 'AI returned malformed XML. Please try again.' })
   171→  }
   172→
   173→  const xmlText = extractTextFromXml(xml)
   174→  const xmlTextLen = xmlText.length
   175→  const divergence = Math.abs(inputLen - xmlTextLen) / inputLen
   176→
   177→  const documentType = detectDocumentType(xml)
   178→
   179→  return { xml, divergence, documentType }
   180→})
   181→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #65
Now I have a complete understanding of the app. Let me install dependencies and start the dev server. Key things I know: 1. Doc type selector: two `<button class="type-btn">` elements - "Resume" and "Letter" 2. Active state: `type-btn--active` class 3. Format button uses NuxtUI's `UButton` component 4. Button label: "Format My Resume" or "Format My Letter" based on `selectedDocType` 5. API sends `docType` as `'resume'` or `'letter'` in the request body 6. Both buttons disabled when `isFormatting` is true 7. Next I'll get the dev environment running and then write the tests for FRG-216.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #75
The dev server is running and responding. Now let me write the Playwright tests for FRG-216. I'll create a new test file specifically for these tests. Based on my analysis of the code: 1. `type-btn` is the CSS class for the type buttons 2. `type-btn--active` is the active state class 3. The buttons have text "Resume" and "Letter" 4. The format button is `UButton` with text derived from `formatButtonLabel` Key things for each test: - **Test 1**: Verify Resume and Letter buttons exist, Resume is active (has `type-btn--active`), Format button says "Format My Resume" - **Test 2**: Click "Letter" button, verify it becomes active while Resume loses the active state, and the format button updates to "Format My Letter" - **Test 3**: Click "Letter" then back to "Resume", verify Resume regains the active state and button text reverts to "Format My Resume" - **Test 4**: Fill the textarea with text and start formatting, then verify both type buttons are disabled during the process - **Test 5 & 6**: For the preview tests with Resume and Letter, I'll need to handle the network requests since we're running locally without an API key—I can use `page.route()` to intercept and mock those calls Now I'm setting up the test file itself, following the same structure and imports as the existing spec file, using the `BASE_URL` environment variable for localhost:3000, and matching the established test patterns. Before I start writing though, I should check whether there's already an `.env.local` file or OpenAI API key configured.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #81
The dev server actually runs on port 3002, not 3000. But the server is running and I confirmed it responded on port 3000... Wait, but I started it with `--port 3000`. Let me re-check. Actually, I started with `-- --port 3000` flag override, so port 3000 should work. And the curl test confirmed it. No API key is configured. For tests 5 and 6, I'll use `page.route()` to intercept and mock the API response. Now let me write the new test file. I'll name it `frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts`. I need to set up tests for the doc type selector component—verifying that the Resume and Letter buttons render correctly with proper active states, and that clicking between them updates both the button styling and the format button text accordingly. Then I'll test that the buttons become disabled while formatting is in progress, and that the API calls work correctly for both document types by intercepting the network requests and returning mocked XML responses that match each format's schema. defined in the server code. Let me write these tests using `page.route()` for the network interception approach for tests 4-6. This avoids dependency on the OpenAI API. For the UButton rendering, looking at the index.vue template, when loading it shows 'Formatting...' text. The UButton displays a spinner and changes text during the loading state. I'll locate it by name matching `/format my resume/i` or `/format my letter/i`. For Test 4, I need to intercept the API route and add a delay so I can verify that both `type-btn` buttons are disabled while the format button is in its loading state. The `isFormatting` state controls this disabling behavior, and since the API request takes time, I can intercept and delay the response to check the disabled state during the request. Now I'll write out the test file.

The `isFormatting` state controls this disabling behavior, and since the API request takes time, I can intercept and delay the response to check the disabled state during the request.\n\nNow I'll write out the test file.",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #85
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts",
  "content": "import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Sample content \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nSUMMARY\nFull-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n\nEXPERIENCE\nSenior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n\u2022 Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users\n\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization\n\nEDUCATION\nB.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n\nSKILLS\nJavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n\nconst LETTER_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nMarch 4, 2026\n\nHiring Manager\nTechVision Inc.\n123 Innovation Drive\nSan Francisco, CA 94102\n\nDear Hiring Manager,\n\nI am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.\nWith seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would\nmake a valuable addition to your team.\n\nThank you for considering my application.\n\nSincerely,\nAlex Rivera`.trim();\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML responses \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n  <experience>\n    <role>\n      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n      <bullets>\n        <bullet>Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization</bullet>\n      </bullets>\n    </role>\n  </experience>\n  <education>\n    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    <year>2019</year>\n  </education>\n  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n</document>`;\n\nconst MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type=\"cover-letter\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <date>March 4, 2026</date>\n  <recipient>\n    <name>Hiring Manager</name>\n    <company>TechVision Inc.</company>\n    <address>San Francisco, CA 94102</address>\n  </recipient>\n  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>\n  <body>\n    <paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.</paragraph>\n    <paragraph>Thank you for considering my application.</paragraph>\n  </body>\n  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>\n  <signature>Alex Rivera</signature>\n</document>`;\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nasync function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n  await textarea.click();\n  await textarea.fill(text);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n}\n\nfunction getResumeButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' });\n}\n\nfunction getLetterButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Letter' });\n}\n\nasync function hasActiveClass(page: Page, button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeButton>): Promise<boolean> {\n  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-216 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// Test 1: Doc type selector renders\ntest('FRG-216 / 1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  // Both buttons must exist\n  await expect(resumeBtn).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(letterBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Resume is active by default\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should have active styling by default').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should NOT be active by default').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 2: Toggle switches to Letter\ntest('FRG-216 / 2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  // Click Letter\n  await letterBtn.click();\n\n  // Letter should become active, Resume should not\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should have active styling after click').toBe(true);\n\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should lose active styling').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label should change\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 3: Toggle switches back to Resume\ntest('FRG-216 / 3. Toggle switches back to Resume', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  // Click Letter, then Resume\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await resumeBtn.click();\n\n  // Resume should be active again\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should be active after switching back').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should be inactive after switching back').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label should revert\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 4: Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\ntest('FRG-216 / 4. Type toggle buttons are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Intercept the API call and delay it so we can check disabled state\n  let resolveRequest: () => void;\n  const requestHeld = new Promise<void>(resolve => { resolveRequest = resolve; });\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    // Hold the request until we've checked the disabled state\n    await requestHeld;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();\n  await formatBtn.click();\n\n  // Wait briefly for the loading state to kick in\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n\n  // Both type buttons must be disabled while formatting\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  await expect(resumeBtn, 'Resume button should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled();\n  await expect(letterBtn, 'Letter button should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled();\n\n  // Release the request and wait for completion\n  resolveRequest!();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n});\n\n// Test 5: Resume formatting sends correct docType and shows resume XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 5. Resume formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, resume XML elements in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const request = route.request();\n    const body = JSON.parse(request.postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  // Resume is selected by default \u2014 verify it\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive).toBe(true);\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i }).click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to show formatted content\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'resume'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"resume\"').toBe('resume');\n\n  // Preview should contain content from the resume XML (name, employer)\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 6: Letter formatting sends correct docType and shows letter XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 6. Letter formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, letter XML elements in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const request = route.request();\n    const body = JSON.parse(request.postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  // Switch to Letter\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible();\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, LETTER_TEXT);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i }).click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to appear\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'letter'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"letter\"').toBe('letter');\n\n  // Preview should contain letter-specific content\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  // Verify letter elements rendered: greeting and closing are in the mock XML\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Dear Hiring Manager/i)).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Sincerely/i)).toBeVisible();\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01MGZZDqucLwTn6WzDvHp6Wb",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts",
      "content": "import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Sample content \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nSUMMARY\nFull-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n\nEXPERIENCE\nSenior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n\u2022 Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users\n\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization\n\nEDUCATION\nB.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n\nSKILLS\nJavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n\nconst LETTER_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nMarch 4, 2026\n\nHiring Manager\nTechVision Inc.\n123 Innovation Drive\nSan Francisco, CA 94102\n\nDear Hiring Manager,\n\nI am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.\nWith seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would\nmake a valuable addition to your team.\n\nThank you for considering my application.\n\nSincerely,\nAlex Rivera`.trim();\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML responses \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n  <experience>\n    <role>\n      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n      <bullets>\n        <bullet>Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization</bullet>\n      </bullets>\n    </role>\n  </experience>\n  <education>\n    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    <year>2019</year>\n  </education>\n  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n</document>`;\n\nconst MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type=\"cover-letter\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <date>March 4, 2026</date>\n  <recipient>\n    <name>Hiring Manager</name>\n    <company>TechVision Inc.</company>\n    <address>San Francisco, CA 94102</address>\n  </recipient>\n  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>\n  <body>\n    <paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.</paragraph>\n    <paragraph>Thank you for considering my application.</paragraph>\n  </body>\n  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>\n  <signature>Alex Rivera</signature>\n</document>`;\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nasync function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n  await textarea.click();\n  await textarea.fill(text);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n}\n\nfunction getResumeButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' });\n}\n\nfunction getLetterButton(page: Page) {\n  return page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Letter' });\n}\n\nasync function hasActiveClass(page: Page, button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeButton>): Promise<boolean> {\n  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-216 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// Test 1: Doc type selector renders\ntest('FRG-216 / 1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  // Both buttons must exist\n  await expect(resumeBtn).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(letterBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Resume is active by default\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should have active styling by default').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should NOT be active by default').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 2: Toggle switches to Letter\ntest('FRG-216 / 2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  // Click Letter\n  await letterBtn.click();\n\n  // Letter should become active, Resume should not\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should have active styling after click').toBe(true);\n\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should lose active styling').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label should change\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 3: Toggle switches back to Resume\ntest('FRG-216 / 3. Toggle switches back to Resume', async ({ page }) => {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  // Click Letter, then Resume\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await resumeBtn.click();\n\n  // Resume should be active again\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should be active after switching back').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should be inactive after switching back').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label should revert\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 4: Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\ntest('FRG-216 / 4. Type toggle buttons are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Intercept the API call and delay it so we can check disabled state\n  let resolveRequest: () => void;\n  const requestHeld = new Promise<void>(resolve => { resolveRequest = resolve; });\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    // Hold the request until we've checked the disabled state\n    await requestHeld;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled();\n  await formatBtn.click();\n\n  // Wait briefly for the loading state to kick in\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n\n  // Both type buttons must be disabled while formatting\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n\n  await expect(resumeBtn, 'Resume button should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled();\n  await expect(letterBtn, 'Letter button should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled();\n\n  // Release the request and wait for completion\n  resolveRequest!();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n});\n\n// Test 5: Resume formatting sends correct docType and shows resume XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 5. Resume formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, resume XML elements in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const request = route.request();\n    const body = JSON.parse(request.postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  // Resume is selected by default \u2014 verify it\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeButton(page);\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(page, resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive).toBe(true);\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i }).click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to show formatted content\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'resume'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"resume\"').toBe('resume');\n\n  // Preview should contain content from the resume XML (name, employer)\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 6: Letter formatting sends correct docType and shows letter XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 6. Letter formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, letter XML elements in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const request = route.request();\n    const body = JSON.parse(request.postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n\n  // Switch to Letter\n  const letterBtn = getLetterButton(page);\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible();\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, LETTER_TEXT);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i }).click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to appear\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'letter'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"letter\"').toBe('letter');\n\n  // Preview should contain letter-specific content\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  // Verify letter elements rendered: greeting and closing are in the mock XML\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Dear Hiring Manager/i)).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Sincerely/i)).toBeVisible();\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #95
I see several issues: 1. **Tests 1, 3, 5**: `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` matches two elements - the type toggle button "Resume" AND the "Format My Resume" button (which seems to have accessibility name "Resume" too). I need to use more specific selectors. 2. **Tests 2**: Letter button click not working - probably because the `getLetterButton` locator matches multiple elements too, or it's matching the disabled format button's accessible name. Wait, actually for test 2 the error says "Letter button should have active styling after click" - let me think. The `Letter` button text-matches to one button but the click might not be working because `getLetterButton` is already matching multiple? Let me look at error 2 more carefully. The real problem is that `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` is picking up both the type selector button and the "Format My Resume" button since they both have "Resume" in their accessible names. I need to scope the selector to the type selector container specifically. For test 4, the format button stays disabled even after filling the textarea, which suggests the button's enabled state isn't properly tied to the textarea content. The issue might be that `fillTextarea` is working but the button's disabled condition isn't being re-evaluated, or there's a reactivity problem with how the button checks if the textarea has content. I see the issue now - `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` is matching multiple buttons because both the type toggle button and the "Format My Resume" button contain "Resume" in their accessible names, and without `exact: true` it matches any partial match. Let me check how Playwright handles this same scenario. I'm realizing that Playwright's `getByRole` with a string name does case-insensitive substring matching for the accessible name, which explains why "Format My Resume" is matching the query for just "Resume". Let me trace through the actual HTML to understand what's happening with the NuxtUI button element. I see the issue now—the Resume button has whitespace around it, so I need to either trim it or use the exact matching approach. Using `{ name: 'Resume', exact: true }` should isolate the type button and prevent it from matching the format button's text. I'll apply the same strategy with `{ name: 'Letter', exact: true }` for consistency, though I'm noticing the letter button test is still failing on the active styling check. The problem might be that Vue's reactivity hasn't updated the DOM by the time the assertion runs, or the click isn't registering on the correct element. Let me check if the `hasActiveClass` function is evaluating the classList properly after the click. Looking at test 4, the format button stays disabled even after filling the textarea, which suggests the fill operation might not be working as expected—the button only enables when there's actual text content, so if the textarea is still empty, that would explain why it remains disabled. The textarea fill with a 300ms timeout should give Vue enough time to react, but the button's still showing as disabled because the text content check is failing. Looking at the UButton structure, the "Format My Resume" text is nested inside Nuxt UI components, so I'm wondering if the role-based selector is even finding the right button when it's enabled. The real issue might be that the `page.route()` setup with an unresolved promise is keeping the button disabled until `resolveRequest` gets called later. The button's disabled state is tied to Vue's reactivity with the `textContent` ref, so when Playwright fills the textarea, it should trigger the `input` event that Vue listens to. But the 90-second timeout suggests the button never becomes enabled at all, which points to a potential issue with how `fill()` interacts with Vue's `v-model` binding—I need to verify whether the input event is actually firing. Looking more closely at test 4, I think I found the real problem: the `page.route()` handler I set up holds onto the request by awaiting a Promise that never gets resolved, which would block the entire request indefinitely. I see the issue now - the route intercept is set up before the page navigation, and the `requestHeld` Promise never resolves until after I check the button state, so any API request during page load gets blocked indefinitely and prevents the button from becoming enabled. Looking at the test failures more carefully, tests 1-3 and 5 are failing due to strict mode violations with multiple matching buttons, while test 4 times out because the textarea fill isn't working properly or the v-model binding isn't updating, and test 6 shows the letter button click isn't updating the format button text. The core issues are the strict mode violations that need scoping and the textarea fill mechanism that's preventing the button from becoming enabled. the Letter button selector should work correctly since it's the only button with that name initially, but there might be a timing issue where Vue hasn't updated the format button text yet after the click. Let me add a wait after clicking the Letter button to give Vue time to process the state change, and I should also check if the format button is being disabled when the textarea is empty on page load. I think the real problem is that `getByRole('button', { name: 'Letter' })` might be matching the "Format My Letter" button instead of the intended one, since Playwright's `getByRole` does exact matching by default but I need to verify how it handles partial matches in the accessible name. Let me check the Playwright documentation more carefully to understand the exact matching behavior for the `name` option. I think I see what's happening—the NuxtUI `UButton` component might be setting an `aria-label` or computing its accessible name differently when it's in a loading or disabled state, so even though the visible text says "Format My Resume", the accessible name that testing libraries see could just be "Resume". Let me trace through how Playwright determines accessible names for buttons to figure out what's actually being exposed. Resume" (with spaces trimmed), and "Resume" also matches as a substring within "Format My Resume" since Playwright's `getByRole` does substring matching by default. I was confused about the exact matching behavior—Playwright's default is actually `exact: true` for case-insensitive matching, but I need to either add `exact: true` to be more specific or scope the selector to the `.type-selector` container to avoid the ambiguity. The error output clearly shows it's matching "Format My Resume" when I'm looking for just "Resume", so something else is going on. The UButton might have a complex ARIA structure with aria-labelledby or computed accessible names from nested elements and icons. Let me examine the actual rendered HTML and how the accessible name is being computed rather than guessing at the selector behavior. I'm switching to a more reliable selector approach using the `.type-selector` container with regex filters to match exact text, which should cleanly target the Resume and Letter buttons. For the format button issue, I'm examining the actual rendered HTML and NuxtUI's button styling to understand why the accessibility name computation might not be matching "Format My Resume" as expected. I think the issue might be that the button's accessible name is computed differently than expected. Let me try scoping the selector to the type-selector container and matching by text content instead, or using a CSS class selector to be more specific. For the format button, I need to figure out why the role-based query isn't finding it. The real problem seems to be that filling the textarea isn't actually working—the button stays disabled even after 90 seconds of waiting, which means the text input never made it into the field. I should try using `pressSequentially` or `page.keyboard.type()` instead of the standard fill method, since there might be a reactivity issue with how Nuxt 3 is handling the input. Looking at test 5 more carefully, it's failing earlier than test 4—it hits a strict mode violation in the `hasActiveClass` check because the button selector is resolving to 2 elements instead of 1, so it never even gets to the fill and click part. For test 4, I'm wondering if the issue is with how I'm handling the request interception—I'm creating a Promise that gets held until after the button check, but if the page makes any API requests during initialization (maybe from Nuxt 3 devtools or some polling), that could cause a deadlock. Let me trace through what actually happens when test 4 runs. The textarea fill with `fillTextarea()` should trigger an `input` event that Vue's `v-model` listens to, so the button should enable once the text is there. But it's staying disabled, which means Vue isn't seeing the text content. I'm starting to think this might be a hydration issue—if Nuxt 3 is doing SSR or has a hydration step in dev mode, the textarea might not be properly connected to Vue's reactivity system when I'm trying to fill it. Even though `page.goto()` waits for the `load` event and I'm clicking the textarea first, the page might still be settling after that. Let me add a wait to ensure hydration is complete before attempting to fill the textarea, and I'll also scope the type button selectors to fix the strict mode violation. Looking at the error more carefully, test 4 is failing because the format button is disabled when it should be enabled. The button selector matches the UButton component correctly, but something about the localhost environment is preventing it from becoming enabled even after text is entered. I'm wondering if Vue's reactivity isn't syncing properly with Playwright's programmatic fill, or if there's a route intercept blocking something that would normally enable the button. Let me check the screenshot to see what's actually happening on the page.

}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #101
Now I can see the actual page! The screenshots reveal several important things: 1. **The page is only showing half the UI** - The "Resume" button is not visible in the screenshot! I can see "Letter" button and "Format My Resume" button at the bottom, but "Resume" button is cut off (outside the viewport). The browser window viewport is too small. 2. **The action bar is at the bottom** - The layout shows: - Textarea (left panel with placeholder text) - At the very bottom: "Letter" button and "Format My Resume" button - The "Resume" button must be to the LEFT of "Letter" but is cut off Looking more carefully, the viewport appears to be showing the right portion of the interface with the preview panel, while the left editor panel and the "Resume" button in the action bar are cut off due to the narrow viewport width. I'm examining the screenshot more carefully to locate the "Resume" button in the type-selector—it should appear to the left of "Letter" at the bottom, but I'm having trouble pinpointing exactly where it is or whether it's being clipped by CSS constraints. The real problem is becoming clearer: the test is failing because `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` is matching both the type-selector button AND the "Format My Resume" action button, causing ambiguity. I need to scope the query to the `.type-selector` container or use a more specific CSS class selector to target only the button I want. For test 4 specifically, the "Format My Resume" button stays disabled even though the textarea should have been filled. The page route is set up before navigation, but that shouldn't block the initial page load since the route pattern `**/api/format` is very specific and wouldn't match the navigation request itself. I'm trying to figure out why the textarea fill isn't working in that test scenario. Looking at the failure screenshot, the textarea is showing placeholder text and the button is disabled, which suggests the fill operation either never happened or something cleared the value afterward. The fill function clicks the textarea and uses the fill method, then waits 300ms, so it should work. But this is the screenshot captured at the 90-second timeout, so if the fill did happen, something must have cleared it or prevented Vue's reactive state from updating properly—possibly a hydration issue with Nuxt. Let me try inspecting the actual page state directly using the browser devtools to see what's really happening with the textarea and the button state. Actually, I'm noticing something critical in the test 2 screenshot - the format button still says "Format My Resume" even after clicking the Letter button, which means Vue's reactive state isn't updating when the click happens. And in test 4, the textarea remains empty with its placeholder text visible even after `fillTextarea` runs, suggesting that method isn't actually working with this Nuxt 3 setup. Both problems point to the same root cause: Vue's event listeners aren't being triggered by Playwright's programmatic interactions, likely because the app hasn't fully hydrated yet. I need to add a proper hydration wait before running the tests - either using `networkidle` or waiting for Vue to be ready. The existing tests work fine against the Vercel deployment, so this is specific to how the local Nuxt 3 dev environment handles hydration. Looking back at the screenshots more carefully, I'm trying to figure out the exact layout of the buttons at the bottom - I can see "Letter" and "Format My Resume" but I need to verify where the "Resume" button is positioned in the type-selector. I'm realizing the Playwright headless browser might be using a different viewport size than expected—let me check the configuration to see what dimensions it's actually rendering at. Looking more carefully at the screenshot, I can see the full page layout, but I'm noticing the action bar positioning doesn't match what I'd expect if the Resume button should be visible before the Letter button in the flex container. visible (left side). Let me look at where the buttons are... At the very bottom left, I can see: - "Letter" button text - "Format My Resume" button But the "Resume" button would be to the LEFT of "Letter" at the same y position. Perhaps the "Resume" button IS there but the screenshot resolution makes it hard to see due to the transparent background. Actually, looking at the screenshot image dimensions - it seems to be a viewport screenshot (not full page), and the resolution looks about right for 1280x720. Oh! I just noticed - in the screenshot, the text "Letter" at the bottom appears to be just the button text, but there should also be "Resume" text to its left. The "Resume" type button has an active state with a dark gray background and white text, while the "Letter" button has a white background with gray text. In the screenshot, I don't see any dark button to the left of "Letter", which suggests either the Resume button isn't visible due to its dark background blending with the edge, the page wasn't fully rendered when captured, or the viewport doesn't show it. Wait, I should reconsider this more carefully. The bottom of the test 4 screenshot shows "Letter" text and the "Format My Resume" button at the bottom left. Since this appears to be the left panel of a split view layout, the editor panel would occupy the left half of the viewport. In the editor panel, the action bar sits at the bottom with two components: the type selector containing Resume and Letter buttons, and the action row with the Format button. Looking at the screenshot around y≈662, I can see "Letter" text which should be in the type selector, but "Resume" should be to its left. At x=20-80, there should be the Resume button with a dark background. The Resume button is probably there with its dark background and white text, but the screenshot might not render it clearly. Let me stop speculating about visibility and focus on the actual code issue I know exists: the strict mode violation where `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` is matching both the button element and something else. I need to scope the query to the `.type-selector` container to fix this. There's also a Vue reactivity issue where clicking the Letter button doesn't update the `selectedDocType` state, so the format button label stays at "Format My Resume". I should use a more reliable interaction approach or ensure the page is fully loaded. The textarea fill also isn't triggering Vue's v-model update, so I might need to use `pressSequentially` or manually trigger the `input` event. Let me check how the existing tests handle this—test `1f` passes against Vercel... Examining how the textarea fill works in the passing test... The key insight is that the dev server needs more time to hydrate because of SSR, client hydration, and HMR. I should add `await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')` after navigation and wait for the textarea to be interactable. For the button selector, I'll scope it to `.type-selector`. The `hasActiveClass` function needs a single element match, so I need to fix the selector. After clicking the Letter button, Vue needs time to process the change, so I'll add a timeout. For test 4, I might try `pressSequentially` instead of `fill` or use `dispatchEvent` to trigger the input event properly. Let me look more closely at the test 4 screenshot to understand what's happening with the format button. The format button is disabled and the textarea is showing placeholder text, which suggests either the `fillTextarea` call never ran, the fill didn't actually set the value, or Vue isn't detecting the change. In Playwright, `fill()` should trigger both `input` and `change` events that Vue's `v-model` listens to, so it should work. But in Nuxt 3 with SSR and hydration, there might be timing issues where Vue hasn't fully initialized when the fill happens. I'm realizing the screenshot at the failure point shows the textarea still empty with placeholder text visible, which is odd because `textarea.fill()` should set the value regardless of Vue reactivity. Let me reconsider whether the fill is actually succeeding or if something else is preventing it from working. I'm wondering if there's an API request happening during page load that's getting intercepted and blocking things, but Nuxt 3 shouldn't be making those calls unless the page explicitly uses `useFetch` or `useAsyncData`. Let me step back and focus on the concrete issues I can fix: tightening up the selector specificity with scoped locators and adding proper network waits to ensure the page is fully loaded before interacting with elements. Looking at test 4 more carefully, I see the real problem now. The route intercept holds the request indefinitely, but I'm checking if the button is enabled before actually resolving that held request. The button check times out at 90 seconds waiting for something that will never happen because the request handler is still hanging. I think the real problem is that Nuxt 3's hydration process happens after the initial page load, so Vue event handlers aren't attached yet when I'm trying to interact with the form. I need to wait for the network to fully settle and the client-side hydration to complete before attempting to fill the form or click buttons. For the textarea input, I'm going to use `pressSequentially` which fires individual keyboard events for each character—this should reliably trigger Vue's `v-model` binding even if the standard `fill()` method doesn't work. Still writing test fixes... Still writing the helper function... For test 4, I'm going to intercept the API call and add a delay so I can check the button state while the request is still in flight, rather than waiting for it to complete. I'll set up the route handler to pause for 10 seconds, then fill the textarea and verify the button remains enabled during that time. Writing helper functions... I need to verify that the selector resolves to exactly one element per button, so the evaluate call will work properly. For the Letter button toggle test, I should add a wait after clicking to give Vue time to process the change. And for the format button, I'm checking that the accessible name matching is precise enough to avoid picking up the Resume type button since both contain the word "resume." Now I'm ready to write out the corrected test file.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #102
Now I understand the issues clearly: 1. `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` does substring matching and hits BOTH the type toggle AND the "Format My Resume" UButton 2. Vue reactivity not updating after click/fill — need `networkidle` wait after goto 3. Test 4's route intercept approach needs a timeout-based delay instead of unresolved Promise Let me fix all these:
▶ VIEW RAW DATA

  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "Now I understand the issues clearly:\n\n1. `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` does substring matching and hits BOTH the type toggle AND the \"Format My Resume\" UButton\n2. Vue reactivity not updating after click/fill \u2014 need `networkidle` wait after goto\n3. Test 4's route intercept approach needs a timeout-based delay instead of unresolved Promise\n\nLet me fix all these:"
  }
}
