Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts",
  "content": "import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Sample content \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nSUMMARY\nFull-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n\nEXPERIENCE\nSenior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n\u2022 Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users\n\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization\n\nEDUCATION\nB.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n\nSKILLS\nJavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n\nconst LETTER_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nMarch 4, 2026\n\nHiring Manager\nTechVision Inc.\n123 Innovation Drive\nSan Francisco, CA 94102\n\nDear Hiring Manager,\n\nI am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.\nWith seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would\nmake a valuable addition to your team.\n\nThank you for considering my application.\n\nSincerely,\nAlex Rivera`.trim();\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML responses \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n  <experience>\n    <role>\n      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n      <bullets>\n        <bullet>Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization</bullet>\n      </bullets>\n    </role>\n  </experience>\n  <education>\n    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    <year>2019</year>\n  </education>\n  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n</document>`;\n\nconst MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type=\"cover-letter\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <date>March 4, 2026</date>\n  <recipient>\n    <name>Hiring Manager</name>\n    <company>TechVision Inc.</company>\n    <address>San Francisco, CA 94102</address>\n  </recipient>\n  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>\n  <body>\n    <paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.</paragraph>\n    <paragraph>Thank you for considering my application.</paragraph>\n  </body>\n  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>\n  <signature>Alex Rivera</signature>\n</document>`;\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/**\n * Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full hydration (including Nuxt 3 client-side\n * mounting) before the test body begins.\n */\nasync function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n  // Ensure the textarea is visible \u2014 confirms Vue has mounted\n  await page.locator('textarea').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });\n}\n\n/**\n * Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue's reactive model to update.\n * Uses click + fill + value verification to avoid intermittent fill() issues.\n */\nasync function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n  await textarea.click();\n  await textarea.fill(text);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  // Verify the value was actually set; retry once if needed\n  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {\n    await textarea.click();\n    await textarea.fill(text);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Scope type-toggle buttons to the .type-selector container to avoid matching\n * the \"Format My Resume\" / \"Format My Letter\" UButton, which also contains\n * the words \"Resume\" and \"Letter\" in its accessible name.\n */\nfunction getResumeToggle(page: Page) {\n  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume' });\n}\n\nfunction getLetterToggle(page: Page) {\n  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Letter' });\n}\n\nasync function hasActiveClass(button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeToggle>): Promise<boolean> {\n  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-216 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// Test 1: Doc type selector renders\ntest('FRG-216 / 1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  // Both toggle buttons must exist\n  await expect(resumeBtn).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(letterBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Resume is active by default\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should have active styling by default').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should NOT be active by default').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label reflects Resume selection\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 2: Toggle switches to Letter\ntest('FRG-216 / 2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  // Click Letter toggle\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300); // allow Vue reactivity\n\n  // Letter should become active, Resume should not\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should have active styling after click').toBe(true);\n\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should lose active styling').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label must change to Letter\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 3: Toggle switches back to Resume\ntest('FRG-216 / 3. Toggle switches back to Resume', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  // Switch to Letter then back to Resume\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  await resumeBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n\n  // Resume should be active again\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should be active after switching back').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should be inactive after switching back').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label must revert\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 4: Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\ntest('FRG-216 / 4. Type toggle buttons are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Intercept and delay the API for 10 seconds \u2014 enough time to verify the disabled state\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10_000));\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n\n  // Both type-toggle buttons must be disabled while API request is in flight\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  await expect(resumeBtn, 'Resume toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await expect(letterBtn, 'Letter toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n});\n\n// Test 5: Resume formatting sends correct docType and shows resume XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 5. Resume formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, resume XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Resume is selected by default\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  expect(await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn), 'Resume toggle should be active by default').toBe(true);\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to replace the placeholder\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'resume'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"resume\"').toBe('resume');\n\n  // Preview should render content from the mocked resume XML\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 6: Letter formatting sends correct docType and shows letter XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 6. Letter formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, letter XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Switch to Letter\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, LETTER_TEXT);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i }).click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to appear\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'letter'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"letter\"').toBe('letter');\n\n  // Preview should contain letter-specific content from the mocked XML\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Dear Hiring Manager/i)).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Sincerely/i)).toBeVisible();\n});\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_01TsMtVdg7p2BvCHdi2fh9nG",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts",
      "content": "import { test, expect, Page } from '@playwright/test';\n\nconst BASE_URL = process.env.BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Sample content \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst RESUME_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 (555) 123-4567 \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nSUMMARY\nFull-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.\n\nEXPERIENCE\nSenior Software Engineer \u2014 Acme Corp (2021\u2013Present)\n\u2022 Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users\n\u2022 Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization\n\nEDUCATION\nB.S. Computer Science \u2014 University of California, Berkeley (2019)\n\nSKILLS\nJavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS`.trim();\n\nconst LETTER_TEXT = `Alex Rivera\nalex.rivera@email.com \u00b7 San Francisco, CA\n\nMarch 4, 2026\n\nHiring Manager\nTechVision Inc.\n123 Innovation Drive\nSan Francisco, CA 94102\n\nDear Hiring Manager,\n\nI am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc.\nWith seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would\nmake a valuable addition to your team.\n\nThank you for considering my application.\n\nSincerely,\nAlex Rivera`.trim();\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Mock XML responses \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\nconst MOCK_RESUME_XML = `<document type=\"resume\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <phone>(555) 123-4567</phone>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <summary>Full-stack software engineer with 7 years of experience building scalable web applications.</summary>\n  <experience>\n    <role>\n      <title>Senior Software Engineer</title>\n      <company>Acme Corp</company>\n      <period>2021\u2013Present</period>\n      <bullets>\n        <bullet>Led development of a microservices architecture serving 2M+ daily active users</bullet>\n        <bullet>Reduced API latency by 40% through caching and query optimization</bullet>\n      </bullets>\n    </role>\n  </experience>\n  <education>\n    <degree>B.S. Computer Science</degree>\n    <school>University of California, Berkeley</school>\n    <year>2019</year>\n  </education>\n  <skills>JavaScript, TypeScript, React, Node.js, Docker, AWS</skills>\n</document>`;\n\nconst MOCK_LETTER_XML = `<document type=\"cover-letter\">\n  <header>\n    <name>Alex Rivera</name>\n    <contact>\n      <email>alex.rivera@email.com</email>\n      <location>San Francisco, CA</location>\n    </contact>\n  </header>\n  <date>March 4, 2026</date>\n  <recipient>\n    <name>Hiring Manager</name>\n    <company>TechVision Inc.</company>\n    <address>San Francisco, CA 94102</address>\n  </recipient>\n  <greeting>Dear Hiring Manager,</greeting>\n  <body>\n    <paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. With seven years of experience building high-performance web applications, I am confident I would make a valuable addition to your team.</paragraph>\n    <paragraph>Thank you for considering my application.</paragraph>\n  </body>\n  <closing>Sincerely,</closing>\n  <signature>Alex Rivera</signature>\n</document>`;\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 Helpers \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n/**\n * Navigate to BASE_URL and wait for full hydration (including Nuxt 3 client-side\n * mounting) before the test body begins.\n */\nasync function gotoAndWait(page: Page): Promise<void> {\n  await page.goto(BASE_URL);\n  await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');\n  // Ensure the textarea is visible \u2014 confirms Vue has mounted\n  await page.locator('textarea').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 15_000 });\n}\n\n/**\n * Reliably fill a textarea and wait for Vue's reactive model to update.\n * Uses click + fill + value verification to avoid intermittent fill() issues.\n */\nasync function fillTextarea(page: Page, text: string): Promise<void> {\n  const textarea = page.locator('textarea');\n  await textarea.click();\n  await textarea.fill(text);\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  // Verify the value was actually set; retry once if needed\n  const value = await textarea.inputValue();\n  if (value.length < 10 && text.length > 10) {\n    await textarea.click();\n    await textarea.fill(text);\n    await page.waitForTimeout(500);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Scope type-toggle buttons to the .type-selector container to avoid matching\n * the \"Format My Resume\" / \"Format My Letter\" UButton, which also contains\n * the words \"Resume\" and \"Letter\" in its accessible name.\n */\nfunction getResumeToggle(page: Page) {\n  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume' });\n}\n\nfunction getLetterToggle(page: Page) {\n  return page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Letter' });\n}\n\nasync function hasActiveClass(button: ReturnType<typeof getResumeToggle>): Promise<boolean> {\n  return button.evaluate(el => el.classList.contains('type-btn--active'));\n}\n\n// \u2500\u2500\u2500 FRG-216 Tests \u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n\n// Test 1: Doc type selector renders\ntest('FRG-216 / 1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  // Both toggle buttons must exist\n  await expect(resumeBtn).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(letterBtn).toBeVisible();\n\n  // Resume is active by default\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should have active styling by default').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should NOT be active by default').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label reflects Resume selection\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 2: Toggle switches to Letter\ntest('FRG-216 / 2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  // Click Letter toggle\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300); // allow Vue reactivity\n\n  // Letter should become active, Resume should not\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should have active styling after click').toBe(true);\n\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should lose active styling').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label must change to Letter\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 3: Toggle switches back to Resume\ntest('FRG-216 / 3. Toggle switches back to Resume', async ({ page }) => {\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  // Switch to Letter then back to Resume\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  await resumeBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n\n  // Resume should be active again\n  const resumeIsActive = await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn);\n  expect(resumeIsActive, 'Resume button should be active after switching back').toBe(true);\n\n  const letterIsActive = await hasActiveClass(letterBtn);\n  expect(letterIsActive, 'Letter button should be inactive after switching back').toBe(false);\n\n  // Format button label must revert\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).not.toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 4: Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\ntest('FRG-216 / 4. Type toggle buttons are disabled while formatting is in progress', async ({ page }) => {\n  // Intercept and delay the API for 10 seconds \u2014 enough time to verify the disabled state\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10_000));\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.01, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n\n  // Both type-toggle buttons must be disabled while API request is in flight\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n\n  await expect(resumeBtn, 'Resume toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await expect(letterBtn, 'Letter toggle should be disabled during formatting').toBeDisabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n});\n\n// Test 5: Resume formatting sends correct docType and shows resume XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 5. Resume formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, resume XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_RESUME_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'resume' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Resume is selected by default\n  const resumeBtn = getResumeToggle(page);\n  expect(await hasActiveClass(resumeBtn), 'Resume toggle should be active by default').toBe(true);\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, RESUME_TEXT);\n  const formatBtn = page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my resume/i });\n  await expect(formatBtn).toBeEnabled({ timeout: 5_000 });\n  await formatBtn.click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to replace the placeholder\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'resume'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"resume\"').toBe('resume');\n\n  // Preview should render content from the mocked resume XML\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('Acme Corp', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n});\n\n// Test 6: Letter formatting sends correct docType and shows letter XML elements\ntest('FRG-216 / 6. Letter formatting \u2014 correct docType sent, letter XML in preview', async ({ page }) => {\n  let capturedDocType: string | null = null;\n\n  await page.route('**/api/format', async route => {\n    const body = JSON.parse(route.request().postData() || '{}');\n    capturedDocType = body.docType;\n    await route.fulfill({\n      status: 200,\n      contentType: 'application/json',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ xml: MOCK_LETTER_XML, divergence: 0.02, documentType: 'cover-letter' }),\n    });\n  });\n\n  await gotoAndWait(page);\n\n  // Switch to Letter\n  const letterBtn = getLetterToggle(page);\n  await letterBtn.click();\n  await page.waitForTimeout(300);\n  await expect(page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i })).toBeVisible({ timeout: 5_000 });\n\n  await fillTextarea(page, LETTER_TEXT);\n  await page.getByRole('button', { name: /format my letter/i }).click();\n\n  // Wait for preview to appear\n  await expect(page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.')).not.toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 });\n\n  // docType sent in request must be 'letter'\n  expect(capturedDocType, 'Request body should contain docType: \"letter\"').toBe('letter');\n\n  // Preview should contain letter-specific content from the mocked XML\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Dear Hiring Manager/i)).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Sincerely/i)).toBeVisible();\n});\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #110
5/6 tests passing now! The issue with test 6 is that `getByText('Alex Rivera')` matches two elements: 1. `<name>Alex Rivera</name>` in the header (inside a `<name>` XML tag) 2. `<signature>Alex Rivera</signature>` in the signature This happens because the mock XML is being rendered as raw XML nodes in the DOM, and both the `<name>` in the header and the `<signature>` contain "Alex Rivera". I need to use a different locator. Instead of checking for "Alex Rivera" (which appears in both the header and signature), let me check for unique content like "TechVision" (recipient company), or be more specific about the locator. I can fix this by using `.first()` to grab just the first match, or switch to checking for more distinctive content that only appears once in the preview.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #119
Still one test failing - `getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })` matches both: 1. `<company>TechVision Inc.</company>` - exact text 2. `<paragraph>I am writing to express my strong interest in the Senior Software Engineer position at TechVision Inc. ...</paragraph>` - contains "TechVision" I see the pattern - the PreviewPanel renders the XML elements directly in the DOM, and text searches with `exact: false` are hitting multiple elements. The XML nodes `<company>`, `<paragraph>`, etc. are actual DOM elements. The fix: use `.first()` for "TechVision" too, or use an exact match for a unique string. I think the cleanest approach is to just use `.first()` on all the multi-match cases, since the real test assertion here is that `capturedDocType === 'letter'` - the content checks are just secondary validations anyway.

Edit TOOL CALL 
{
  "replace_all": false,
  "file_path": "/home/gem/project/tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts",
  "old_string": "  // Preview should contain letter-specific content from the mocked XML.\n  // 'Alex Rivera' appears in both <name> and <signature> nodes, so use first().\n  await expect(page.getByText('Alex Rivera', { exact: false }).first()).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText('TechVision', { exact: false })).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Dear Hiring Manager/i)).toBeVisible();\n  await expect(page.getByText(/Sincerely/i)).toBeVisible();",
  "new_string": "  // Verify letter-specific content in the rendered preview.\n  // Some strings appear in multiple XML nodes (name/signature, company/paragraph)\n  // so we assert on the full body text rather than individual locators.\n  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain sender name').toMatch(/Alex Rivera/);\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain recipient company').toMatch(/TechVision/);\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain greeting').toMatch(/Dear Hiring Manager/i);\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain closing').toMatch(/Sincerely/i);"
}

      "new_string": "  // Verify letter-specific content in the rendered preview.\n  // Some strings appear in multiple XML nodes (name/signature, company/paragraph)\n  // so we assert on the full body text rather than individual locators.\n  const bodyText = await page.locator('body').innerText();\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain sender name').toMatch(/Alex Rivera/);\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain recipient company').toMatch(/TechVision/);\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain greeting').toMatch(/Dear Hiring Manager/i);\n  expect(bodyText, 'Preview should contain closing').toMatch(/Sincerely/i);"

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #128
Now I see the issue clearly. The body text shows: ``` "OhMyDoc OhMyDoc Export PDF Works best in Chrome. In the print dialog, choose 'Save as PDF'. Resume Letter Format My Letter· Preparing preview…· Your content has been formatted but not rewritten. Please review before exporting." ``` The body text shows "Preparing preview…" - which means the PreviewPanel is still loading/processing when I grab the body text. The preview hasn't rendered the XML content yet.The issue is a timing problem. The `page.getByText('Your formatted document will appear here.').not.toBeVisible()` check passes (placeholder disappears), but the preview panel renders "Preparing preview…" first before showing the actual content.I need to wait for the "Preparing preview..." text to also disappear, or wait for specific content to appear.Let me check the PreviewPanel component to understand its loading states.

USERMESSAGE #131
✓ TOOL RESULT
SUCCESS 
     1→<template>
     2→  <div class="preview-panel">
     3→    <!-- Error Display -->
     4→    <UAlert
     5→      v-if="error"
     6→      color="red"
     7→      variant="solid"
     8→      title="Rendering Error"
     9→      :description="error"
    10→      icon="i-heroicons-exclamation-triangle"
    11→      class="error-alert"
    12→    />
    13→
    14→    <!-- Document Preview — content is DOMPurify-sanitized before assignment to sanitizedHtml -->
    15→    <!-- eslint-disable vue/no-v-html -->
    16→    <div
    17→      v-else-if="sanitizedHtml"
    18→      class="preview-container document-render"
    19→      :style="containerStyle"
    20→      v-html="sanitizedHtml"
    21→    />
    22→    <!-- eslint-enable vue/no-v-html -->
    23→
    24→    <!-- Placeholder State -->
    25→    <div v-else class="placeholder-state">
    26→      <p>Preparing preview…</p>
    27→    </div>
    28→  </div>
    29→</template>
    30→
    31→<script setup lang="ts">
    32→import { ref, computed, watch } from 'vue'
    33→import './resume-styles.css'
    34→
    35→defineOptions({ name: 'PreviewPanel' })
    36→
    37→interface Props {
    38→  xmlContent: string
    39→  zoom?: number
    40→}
    41→
    42→const props = withDefaults(defineProps<Props>(), { zoom: 1 })
    43→
    44→const sanitizedHtml = ref<string>('')
    45→const error = ref<string | undefined>(undefined)
    46→
    47→const containerStyle = computed(() => ({
    48→  transform: `scale(${props.zoom})`,
    49→  transformOrigin: 'top left',
    50→}))
    51→
    52→const ALLOWED_TAGS = [
    53→  // Resume elements
    54→  'document', 'header', 'name', 'contact', 'email', 'phone', 'location',
    55→  'linkedin', 'website', 'summary', 'experience', 'role', 'title', 'company',
    56→  'period', 'bullets', 'bullet', 'education', 'degree', 'school', 'year',
    57→  'note', 'skills', 'certifications', 'projects', 'volunteer',
    58→  // Cover-letter elements
    59→  'date', 'recipient', 'greeting', 'body', 'paragraph', 'closing', 'signature',
    60→  // Standard HTML tags DOMPurify allows anyway — kept for section labels
    61→  'section', 'h2', 'p', 'ul', 'li', 'a', 'br', 'strong', 'em',
    62→]
    63→
    64→const ALLOWED_ATTR = ['type', 'href']
    65→
    66→async function updatePreview() {
    67→  error.value = undefined
    68→  sanitizedHtml.value = ''
    69→
    70→  if (!props.xmlContent) return
    71→
    72→  try {
    73→    // DOMPurify is browser-only — import lazily to avoid SSR issues
    74→    const DOMPurify = (await import('dompurify')).default
    75→    const clean = DOMPurify.sanitize(props.xmlContent, {
    76→      ADD_TAGS: ALLOWED_TAGS,
    77→      ADD_ATTR: ALLOWED_ATTR,
    78→      FORCE_BODY: false,
    79→    })
    80→    sanitizedHtml.value = clean
    81→  }
    82→  catch (err) {
    83→    error.value = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Could not render document'
    84→  }
    85→}
    86→
    87→watch(() => props.xmlContent, updatePreview, { immediate: true })
    88→</script>
    89→
    90→<style scoped>
    91→.preview-panel {
    92→  width: 100%;
    93→  height: 100%;
    94→  overflow: auto;
    95→  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);
    96→  padding: 1.5rem 1.25rem;
    97→}
    98→
    99→.error-alert {
   100→  margin-bottom: 1rem;
   101→}
   102→
   103→.placeholder-state {
   104→  display: flex;
   105→  align-items: center;
   106→  justify-content: center;
   107→  min-height: 200px;
   108→  color: var(--color-gray-600);
   109→}
   110→
   111→.preview-container {
   112→  width: fit-content;
   113→  min-width: 100%;
   114→  padding-bottom: 2rem;
   115→}
   116→
   117→@media print {
   118→  .preview-panel {
   119→    overflow: visible;
   120→    height: auto;
   121→    background: transparent;
   122→    padding: 0;
   123→  }
   124→
   125→  .preview-container {
   126→    transform: none !important;
   127→    width: 100%;
   128→    padding-bottom: 0;
   129→  }
   130→}
   131→</style>
   132→

document-render\"\n    19\u2192      :style=\"containerStyle\"\n    20\u2192      v-html=\"sanitizedHtml\"\n    21\u2192    />\n    22\u2192    <!-- eslint-enable vue/no-v-html -->\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192    <!-- Placeholder State -->\n    25\u2192    <div v-else class=\"placeholder-state\">\n    26\u2192      <p>Preparing preview\u2026</p>\n    27\u2192    </div>\n    28\u2192  </div>\n    29\u2192</template>\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192<script setup lang=\"ts\">\n    32\u2192import { ref, computed, watch } from 'vue'\n    33\u2192import './resume-styles.css'\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192defineOptions({ name: 'PreviewPanel' })\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192interface Props {\n    38\u2192  xmlContent: string\n    39\u2192  zoom?: number\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192const props = withDefaults(defineProps<Props>(), { zoom: 1 })\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192const sanitizedHtml = ref<string>('')\n    45\u2192const error = ref<string | undefined>(undefined)\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192const containerStyle = computed(() => ({\n    48\u2192  transform: `scale(${props.zoom})`,\n    49\u2192  transformOrigin: 'top left',\n    50\u2192}))\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192const ALLOWED_TAGS = [\n    53\u2192  // Resume elements\n    54\u2192  'document', 'header', 'name', 'contact', 'email', 'phone', 'location',\n    55\u2192  'linkedin', 'website', 'summary', 'experience', 'role', 'title', 'company',\n    56\u2192  'period', 'bullets', 'bullet', 'education', 'degree', 'school', 'year',\n    57\u2192  'note', 'skills', 'certifications', 'projects', 'volunteer',\n    58\u2192  // Cover-letter elements\n    59\u2192  'date', 'recipient', 'greeting', 'body', 'paragraph', 'closing', 'signature',\n    60\u2192  // Standard HTML tags DOMPurify allows anyway \u2014 kept for section labels\n    61\u2192  'section', 'h2', 'p', 'ul', 'li', 'a', 'br', 'strong', 'em',\n    62\u2192]\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192const ALLOWED_ATTR = ['type', 'href']\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192async function updatePreview() {\n    67\u2192  error.value = undefined\n    68\u2192  sanitizedHtml.value = ''\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192  if (!props.xmlContent) return\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192  try {\n    73\u2192    // DOMPurify is browser-only \u2014 import lazily to avoid SSR issues\n    74\u2192    const DOMPurify = (await import('dompurify')).default\n    75\u2192    const clean = DOMPurify.sanitize(props.xmlContent, {\n    76\u2192      ADD_TAGS: ALLOWED_TAGS,\n    77\u2192      ADD_ATTR: ALLOWED_ATTR,\n    78\u2192      FORCE_BODY: false,\n    79\u2192    })\n    80\u2192    sanitizedHtml.value = clean\n    81\u2192  }\n    82\u2192  catch (err) {\n    83\u2192    error.value = err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Could not render document'\n    84\u2192  }\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192watch(() => props.xmlContent, updatePreview, { immediate: true })\n    88\u2192</script>\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192<style scoped>\n    91\u2192.preview-panel {\n    92\u2192  width: 100%;\n    93\u2192  height: 100%;\n    94\u2192  overflow: auto;\n    95\u2192  background-color: var(--color-gray-50);\n    96\u2192  padding: 1.5rem 1.25rem;\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192.error-alert {\n   100\u2192  margin-bottom: 1rem;\n   101\u2192}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192.placeholder-state {\n   104\u2192  display: flex;\n   105\u2192  align-items: center;\n   106\u2192  justify-content: center;\n   107\u2192  min-height: 200px;\n   108\u2192  color: var(--color-gray-600);\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192.preview-container {\n   112\u2192  width: fit-content;\n   113\u2192  min-width: 100%;\n   114\u2192  padding-bottom: 2rem;\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192@media print {\n   118\u2192  .preview-panel {\n   119\u2192    overflow: visible;\n   120\u2192    height: auto;\n   121\u2192    background: transparent;\n   122\u2192    padding: 0;\n   123\u2192  }\n   124\u2192\n   125\u2192  .preview-container {\n   126\u2192    transform: none !important;\n   127\u2192    width: 100%;\n   128\u2192    padding-bottom: 0;\n   129\u2192  }\n   130\u2192}\n   131\u2192</style>\n   132\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
  }
}
ASSISTANTMESSAGE #132
I see! The PreviewPanel has a "Preparing preview…" state that shows while `sanitizedHtml` is empty (during the async DOMPurify import and sanitization). This is an intermediate state between "Your formatted document will appear here." (initial state) and the actual rendered content. The flow is: 1. Initially: `formattedXml` is empty → index.vue shows "Your formatted document will appear here." 2. After API response: `formattedXml` is set → PreviewPanel renders with the XML content 3. PreviewPanel: `sanitizedHtml` starts as '' while DOMPurify loads → shows "Preparing preview…" 4. After DOMPurify loads and sanitizes: `sanitizedHtml` is set → shows rendered content So I need to wait for both the "Preparing preview…" message to disappear and the actual content to appear. Looking at the test output, I can see "Preparing preview…" is currently visible in the body text, which means the preview is still loading. My check for "Your formatted document will appear here." won't work since that's already been replaced by the loading state. The solution is to wait for "Preparing preview…" to disappear with a longer timeout, ensuring the content has fully rendered before the test continues.

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #147
Bash TOOL CALL 
{
  "command": "git add tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts && git commit -m \"$(cat <<'EOF'\nAdd FRG-216 Playwright tests for doc type selector\n\nCovers all 6 test scenarios using page.route() to mock the /api/format\nAPI, making tests independent of OpenAI availability:\n\n1. Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default\n2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update\n3. Toggle switches back to Resume\n4. Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\n5. Resume formatting sends docType:\"resume\", preview shows XML content\n6. Letter formatting sends docType:\"letter\", preview shows letter XML\n\nKey implementation notes:\n- Scope type-btn selectors to button.type-btn to avoid matching the\n  \"Format My Resume\"/\"Format My Letter\" UButton (substring collision)\n- Wait for networkidle after goto to ensure Nuxt 3 hydration completes\n- In test 4, use a 10s delayed route rather than an unresolved promise\n- Wait for \"Preparing preview\u2026\" to clear (async DOMPurify step in\n  PreviewPanel) before asserting on rendered content in tests 5-6\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",
  "description": "Commit FRG-216 test file"
}

Doc type selector renders with Resume active by default\n2. Toggle switches to Letter \u2014 button and label update\n3. Toggle switches back to Resume\n4. Type toggle buttons disabled during formatting\n5. Resume formatting sends docType:\"resume\", preview shows XML content\n6. Letter formatting sends docType:\"letter\", preview shows letter XML\n\nKey implementation notes:\n- Scope type-btn selectors to button.type-btn to avoid matching the\n  \"Format My Resume\"/\"Format My Letter\" UButton (substring collision)\n- Wait for networkidle after goto to ensure Nuxt 3 hydration completes\n- In test 4, use a 10s delayed route rather than an unresolved promise\n- Wait for \"Preparing preview\u2026\" to clear (async DOMPurify step in\n  PreviewPanel) before asserting on rendered content in tests 5-6\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>\nEOF\n)\"",

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #156
Write TOOL CALL 
{
  "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "content": "# OhMyDoc QA Project Memory\n\n## Project\n- QA repo: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ohmydoc-qa.git (at /home/gem/project)\n- Source app repo URL in `.env`: SOURCE_REPO / SOURCE_BRANCH\n- Tests are Playwright (TypeScript), run with `BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test`\n- Source app is Nuxt 3 + NuxtUI; dev server starts with `npm run dev -- --port 3000`\n\n## Known Playwright Pitfalls (Nuxt 3 app)\n\n1. **Hydration delay**: Always use `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` + wait for textarea\n   after `page.goto()` \u2014 Nuxt 3 attaches Vue event listeners after the `load` event.\n\n2. **UButton selector ambiguity**: `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` does\n   substring matching and hits BOTH the `button.type-btn` toggle AND the\n   \"Format My Resume\" UButton. Fix: scope to `button.type-btn` class or\n   `.type-selector` container.\n\n3. **PreviewPanel async state**: After API response, PreviewPanel shows\n   \"Preparing preview\u2026\" briefly (async DOMPurify import). Wait for BOTH\n   \"Your formatted document will appear here.\" AND \"Preparing preview\u2026\" to\n   disappear before asserting rendered content.\n\n4. **XML nodes cause getByText strict violations**: The preview renders XML tags\n   as DOM nodes (`<name>`, `<paragraph>`, etc.). Text like \"Alex Rivera\" appears\n   in both `<name>` and `<signature>`. Use `.first()` or assert on `body.innerText()`.\n\n5. **Route intercept for disabled-state tests**: Use `setTimeout(r, 10_000)` inside\n   the route handler to hold the response (not an unresolved Promise \u2014 the Promise\n   approach prevents `resolveRequest!()` from running when the test fails).\n\n## Test File Conventions\n- `gotoAndWait(page)` \u2014 goto + networkidle + textarea visible\n- `fillTextarea(page, text)` \u2014 click + fill + waitForTimeout(300) + retry\n- Type toggle buttons: `page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume/Letter' })`\n- `hasActiveClass(button)` \u2014 checks for `type-btn--active` CSS class\n- All API tests use `page.route('**/api/format', ...)` mock (no OpenAI key needed)\n"
}
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "tool_use",
    "id": "toolu_011UJxppmavGxn3UzuKWJt56",
    "name": "Write",
    "input": {
      "file_path": "/home/gem/.claude/projects/-home-gem-project/memory/MEMORY.md",
      "content": "# OhMyDoc QA Project Memory\n\n## Project\n- QA repo: https://github.com/chillkimtest-oss/ohmydoc-qa.git (at /home/gem/project)\n- Source app repo URL in `.env`: SOURCE_REPO / SOURCE_BRANCH\n- Tests are Playwright (TypeScript), run with `BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000 npx playwright test`\n- Source app is Nuxt 3 + NuxtUI; dev server starts with `npm run dev -- --port 3000`\n\n## Known Playwright Pitfalls (Nuxt 3 app)\n\n1. **Hydration delay**: Always use `waitForLoadState('networkidle')` + wait for textarea\n   after `page.goto()` \u2014 Nuxt 3 attaches Vue event listeners after the `load` event.\n\n2. **UButton selector ambiguity**: `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` does\n   substring matching and hits BOTH the `button.type-btn` toggle AND the\n   \"Format My Resume\" UButton. Fix: scope to `button.type-btn` class or\n   `.type-selector` container.\n\n3. **PreviewPanel async state**: After API response, PreviewPanel shows\n   \"Preparing preview\u2026\" briefly (async DOMPurify import). Wait for BOTH\n   \"Your formatted document will appear here.\" AND \"Preparing preview\u2026\" to\n   disappear before asserting rendered content.\n\n4. **XML nodes cause getByText strict violations**: The preview renders XML tags\n   as DOM nodes (`<name>`, `<paragraph>`, etc.). Text like \"Alex Rivera\" appears\n   in both `<name>` and `<signature>`. Use `.first()` or assert on `body.innerText()`.\n\n5. **Route intercept for disabled-state tests**: Use `setTimeout(r, 10_000)` inside\n   the route handler to hold the response (not an unresolved Promise \u2014 the Promise\n   approach prevents `resolveRequest!()` from running when the test fails).\n\n## Test File Conventions\n- `gotoAndWait(page)` \u2014 goto + networkidle + textarea visible\n- `fillTextarea(page, text)` \u2014 click + fill + waitForTimeout(300) + retry\n- Type toggle buttons: `page.locator('button.type-btn').filter({ hasText: 'Resume/Letter' })`\n- `hasActiveClass(button)` \u2014 checks for `type-btn--active` CSS class\n- All API tests use `page.route('**/api/format', ...)` mock (no OpenAI key needed)\n"
    },
    "caller": {
      "type": "direct"
    }
  }
}

ASSISTANTMESSAGE #158
--- ## Status DONE ## What Changed - `tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts` — New file with 6 Playwright tests covering the doc type selector feature (FRG-216), all running against the local dev server with mocked API responses ## Key Decisions - **Scoped type-btn selectors** (`button.type-btn`): `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` does substring matching and also hits the "Format My Resume" UButton — scoping to the CSS class avoids the strict mode violation - **`networkidle` after goto**: Nuxt 3 attaches Vue reactivity handlers after the `load` event; without this wait, clicks and fills don't trigger `v-model` updates - **10s route delay for test 4**: Replaced the unresolved-Promise intercept with a `setTimeout` delay so the test can proceed normally if the format button check times out - **Body text assertion for test 6**: The PreviewPanel renders XML as DOM nodes (`<name>`, `<signature>`, `<paragraph>`) — "Alex Rivera" and "TechVision" each appear in multiple nodes, so `getByText` strict mode violations are avoided by asserting on `body.innerText()` instead - **"Preparing preview…" wait**: PreviewPanel lazily imports DOMPurify (browser-only), showing a brief intermediate state after the API responds — both placeholder texts must be waited out before asserting rendered content ## PR / Branch Committed directly to `main` (eb09ff4)
▶ VIEW RAW DATA 
{
  "content": {
    "type": "text",
    "text": "---\n\n## Status\nDONE\n\n## What Changed\n- `tests/frg-216-doc-type-selector.spec.ts` \u2014 New file with 6 Playwright tests covering the doc type selector feature (FRG-216), all running against the local dev server with mocked API responses\n\n## Key Decisions\n- **Scoped type-btn selectors** (`button.type-btn`): `getByRole('button', { name: 'Resume' })` does substring matching and also hits the \"Format My Resume\" UButton \u2014 scoping to the CSS class avoids the strict mode violation\n- **`networkidle` after goto**: Nuxt 3 attaches Vue reactivity handlers after the `load` event; without this wait, clicks and fills don't trigger `v-model` updates\n- **10s route delay for test 4**: Replaced the unresolved-Promise intercept with a `setTimeout` delay so the test can proceed normally if the format button check times out\n- **Body text assertion for test 6**: The PreviewPanel renders XML as DOM nodes (`<name>`, `<signature>`, `<paragraph>`) \u2014 \"Alex Rivera\" and \"TechVision\" each appear in multiple nodes, so `getByText` strict mode violations are avoided by asserting on `body.innerText()` instead\n- **\"Preparing preview\u2026\" wait**: PreviewPanel lazily imports DOMPurify (browser-only), showing a brief intermediate state after the API responds \u2014 both placeholder texts must be waited out before asserting rendered content\n\n## PR / Branch\nCommitted directly to `main` (eb09ff4)"
  }
}
